text: Check whether Windows Update is running in the background - it'll quietly eat your bandwidth.
label: helpful_answer

text: Try changing your DNS servers to Google's (8.8.8.8 and 8.8.4.4). You could also run a VPN trial to test whether your ISP is throttling you.

text: I'm posting this as a streaming device issue because after 7 days of troubleshooting by several people there's still no fix. The YouTube app fails on my Fire TV Sticks (4K, 1st and 2nd gen) - it won't load, or loads with no content, or won't play. Login and activation also fail. It survives resets, reinstalls, network tests and ISP checks, but YouTube works fine on my MacBook, laptops and Android phone.
label: help_request

text: YouTube pulled the original app off the Fire Stick for some reason, then brought it back as YouTube TV. Try finding an alternative YouTube app, or use the web browser version via the Fire Stick's HTML5 support.
label: helpful_answer

text: Yeah, there are so many problems with YouTube on the Fire TV stick. I don't get how these big tech giants let this happen.
label: noise

text: My streaming devices (an HTPC and a Raspberry Pi) access my NAS, and all of a sudden the media players can't stream a single thing, including files on the NAS. The menus load fine, other devices can reach the files normally, and modem/router resets and cache clearing did nothing.
label: help_request

text: Fixed it - disabling UPnP on my device seems to have solved the problem. Could someone help me understand why UPnP would mess things up like that?
label: help_request

text: Which device did you disable UPnP on, and what OS is it running? Honestly UPnP has well-documented security vulnerabilities and it's worth asking why it's needed at all - here are some links on the risks and its role in DDoS amplification attacks.
label: helpful_answer

text: I want one dedicated PC in my house to stream movies wirelessly to my other devices, but I don't have internet. What do I need to do to make the PC visible to the computers and TVs? Is it as simple as buying a router and setting it up as if I had internet, or is there more to it?
label: help_request

text: I have a setup like that at home. All you really need is a wireless router. You could even get a 3G/4G router so you can add mobile broadband later.
label: helpful_answer

text: I forgot to mention - you'll also need a DLNA server installed on the PC, like Mezzmo or Serviio, or something more advanced like Plex.
label: helpful_answer
